TXT, MD, DOCX, PDF, SRT, VTT PDFs need selectable text Scanned PDFs need OCR elsewhere 0 chars · ~0 tokens · 0 non-empty lines Load validation TXT Load validation MD Load validation DOCX Load validation PDF Load validation VTT Load validation SRT Detect speaker labels (e.g. David:) Process transcript DETECTED STRUCTURE Paste transcript text to preview speaker labels, paragraph blocks, and line count befo
checks.finalTextStart: Skip to main content yentl Library Paused · 00:01 Overview Source Transcript Claims · 0 Markers · 0 Sources Room Save Export End Speaker 1 Speaker 2 Yentl session workspace Live capture stopped YENTL OPINION · REFRESHING… The validation panel has a budget claim, a context challenge, and a separate school-platform warning. The strongest meta-read is cautious: one speaker asserts a number and uses an urgent frame, while the other asks for the missing evidence and scope. META-READ Still forming Updating while checking Across the current live window, Yentl does not yet have enough clean evidence for a confident conversation-level read. Evidence: Thin sources Scope: Live window Main uncertainty i

The meta-view is that the document itself raises the right challenge: do not accept the percentage or tax frame until the baseline year and actual budget summary are named. checks.initialTextStart: Skip to main content Yentl session workspace Live capture stopped Back to sources Quick claim check Check one specific claim Use this when you already know the claim you want checked. Add context only to clarify who, where, or what the claim is about. Claim Optional context or source note 0 / 2,000 characters Context is for disambiguation, not automatic evidence. Load validation claim Check claim GOOD QUICK CHECKS A factual sentence with names, dates, numbers, or places. A quote or statistic that can be compared against sources. Enough context to distinguish one person, place, or event from another. Quick checks are not a substitute for reviewing a whole source. Use text, file, YouTube, medi
checks.finalTextStart: Skip to main content yentl Library Paused · 00:00 Overview Source Transcript Claims · 1 Markers · 0 Sources Room Save Export End Paused Overview No reliable backing 34/100 “City spending rose by twelve percent this year without raising taxes.” CONFIDENCE 34 / 100 WHY THIS VERDICT The imported brief states the spending claim, but the same exchange asks for the source and baseline year. Yentl should keep it unresolved until the actual budget summary and tax record are named. ● missing baseline year ● missing budget document ● tax claim needs source EVIDENCE STATUS No reliable backing found Confirmed search The confirmed pass did not return reliable source backing. That is not the same as sayin
